G2164.strongs_def: to bear well, i.e. be fertile

G2146.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4383 (πρόσωπον);
G2146.strongs_def: to be of good countenance, i.e. (figuratively) to make a display

G2141.derivation: from a compound of G2090 (ἑτοιμάζω) and the base of G4197 (πορεία);
G2141.strongs_def: (intransitively) to be good for passing through, i.e. (figuratively) have pecuniary means

G2107.derivation: from a presumed compound of G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G1380 (δοκέω);
G2107.strongs_def: satisfaction, i.e. (subjectively) delight, or (objectively) kindness, wish, purpose
G2107.kjv_def: desire, good pleasure (will), X seem good

G2154.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω);

G2170.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5483 (χαρίζομαι);

G2197.strongs_def: Zacharias (i.e. Zechariah), the name of two Israelites

G2194.strongs_def: Zabulon (i.e. Zebulon), a region of Palestine

G2151.strongs_def: to be pious, i.e. (towards God) to worship, or (towards parents) to respect (support)

G2123.derivation: comparative of a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G2873 (κόπος);
G2123.strongs_def: better for toil, i.e. more facile

G2105.derivation: feminine from G2095 (εὖ) and the alternate of G2203 (Ζεύς) (as the god of the weather);
G2105.strongs_def: a clear sky, i.e. fine weather

G2112.strongs_def: directly, i.e. at once or soon
G2112.kjv_def: anon, as soon as, forthwith, immediately, shortly, straightway

G2161.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5177 (τυγχάνω);
G2161.strongs_def: well- fated, i.e. fortunate; Eutychus, a young man

G2193.strongs_def: a conjunction, preposition and adverb of continuance, until (of time and place)
G2193.kjv_def: even (until, unto), (as) far (as), how long, (un-)til(-l), (hither-, un-, up) to, while(-s)

G2148.strongs_def: a storm from the East (or southeast), i.e. (in modern phrase) a Levanter

G2175.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G3605 (ὄζω);

G2186.strongs_def: to stand upon, i.e. be present (in various applications, friendly or otherwise, usually literal);
G2186.kjv_def: --assault, come (in, to, unto, upon), be at hand (instant), present, stand (before, by, over)

G2187.derivation: of Hebrew origin (H0669 or better H06085);

G2182.derivation: from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί) and G2147 (εὑρίσκω);

G2113.strongs_def: to lay a straight course, i.e. sail direct

G2110.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον);
G2110.strongs_def: a worker of good, i.e. (specially) a philanthropist

G2125.strongs_def: to be circumspect, i.e. (by implication) to be apprehensive; religiously, to reverence

G2124.strongs_def: properly, caution, i.e. (religiously) reverence (piety); by implication, dread (concretely)

G2189.strongs_def: hostility; by implication, a reason for opposition

G2130.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a presumed derivative of G3330 (μεταδίδωμι);

G2176.strongs_def: properly, well-named (good-omened), i.e. the left (which was the lucky side among the pagan Greeks); neuter as adverbial, at the left hand

G2180.strongs_def: an Ephesian or inhabitant of Ephesus

G2136.strongs_def: fine travelling; Euodia, a Christian woman

G2111.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι);

G2129.strongs_def: fine speaking, i.e. elegance of language; commendation ("eulogy"), i.e. (reverentially) adoration; religiously, benediction; by implication, consecration; by extension, benefit or largess
G2129.kjv_def: blessing (a matter of) bounty (X -tifully), fair speech

G2117.strongs_def: straight, i.e. (literally) level, or (figuratively) true; adverbially (of time) at once
G2117.kjv_def: anon, by and by, forthwith, immediately, straightway
G2117.usage: straight(5x), right(3x), immediately(3x), straightway(2x), anon(1x), forthwith(1x)

G2181.strongs_def: Ephesus, a city of Asia Minor

G2119.strongs_def: to have good time, i.e. opportunity or leisure

G2106.strongs_def: to think well of, i.e. approve (an act); specially, to approbate (a person or thing)
G2106.kjv_def: think good, (be well) please(-d), be the good (have, take) pleasure, be willing

G2192.strongs_def: to hold (used in very various applications, literally or figuratively, direct or remote; such as possession; ability, contiuity, relation, or condition)
G2192.kjv_def: be (able, X hold, possessed with), accompany, + begin to amend, can(+ -not), X conceive, count, diseased, do + eat, + enjoy, + fear, following, have, hold, keep, + lack, + go to law, lie, + must needs, + of necessity, + need, next, + recover, + reign, + rest, + return, X sick, take for, + tremble, + uncircumcised, use

G2165.strongs_def: to put (middle voice or passively, be) in a good frame of mind, i.e. rejoice
G2165.kjv_def: fare, make glad, be (make) merry, rejoice

G2168.strongs_def: to be grateful, i.e. (actively) to express gratitude (towards); specially, to say grace at a meal

G2114.strongs_def: to cheer up, i.e. (intransitively) be cheerful; neuter comparative (adverbially) more cheerfully

G2159.derivation: adverb from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of (to stretch);
G2159.strongs_def: in a well-strung manner, i.e. (figuratively) intensely (in a good sense, cogently; in a bad one, fiercely)

G2104.strongs_def: well born, i.e. (literally) high in rank, or (figuratively) generous

G2191.strongs_def: an adder or other poisonous snake (literally or figuratively)

G2183.strongs_def: diurnality, i.e. (specially) the quotidian rotation or class of the Jewish priests' service at the Temple, as distributed by families

G2115.kjv_def: of good cheer, the more cheerfully

G2139.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of a presumed compound of G4012 (περί) and G2476 (ἵστημι);
G2139.strongs_def: well standing around, i.e. (a competitor) thwarting (a racer) in every direction (figuratively, of sin in genitive case)

G2172.derivation: middle voice of a primary verb;
G2172.strongs_def: to wish; by implication, to pray to God

G2132.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3563 (νοῦς);
G2132.strongs_def: to be well- minded, i.e. reconcile

G2190.strongs_def: hateful (passively, odious, or actively, hostile); usually as a noun, an adversary (especially Satan)

G2169.strongs_def: gratitude; actively, grateful language (to God, as an act of worship)

G2127.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3056 (λόγος);
G2127.strongs_def: to speak well of, i.e. (religiously) to bless (thank or invoke a benediction upon, prosper)

G2171.strongs_def: properly, a wish, expressed as a petition to God, or in votive obligation

G2143.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4241 (πρέπω);

G2160.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of the base of G5157 (τροπή) (meaning well-turned, i.e. ready at repartee, jocose);
G2160.strongs_def: witticism, i.e. (in a vulgar sense) ribaldry

G2147.derivation: a prolonged form of a primary , which (together with another cognate form) is used for it in all the tenses except the present and imperfect;

G2144.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G4327 (προσδέχομαι);

G2174.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G5590 (ψυχή);
G2174.strongs_def: to be in good spirits, i.e. feel encouraged

G2145.derivation: from G2095 (εὖ) and the same as G4332 (προσεδρεύω);
G2145.strongs_def: sitting well towards, i.e. (figuratively) assiduous (neuter, diligent service)

G2140.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G4160 (ποιέω);

G2135.strongs_def: a castrated person (such being employed in Oriental bed-chambers); by extension an impotent or unmarried man; by implication, a chamberlain (state-officer)

G2185.derivation: from G1909 (ἐπί) and a cognate of G2240 (ἥκω);
G2185.strongs_def: to arrive upon, i.e. extend to

G2137.derivation: from a compound of G2095 (εὖ) and G3598 (ὁδός);
G2137.strongs_def: to help on the road, i.e. (passively) succeed in reaching; figuratively, to succeed in business affairs
